SEARCH
Package org.javalabs.jpa.txn

Interface LitePersistenceContext

All Known Implementing Classes:
PersistenceContextImpl
public interface LitePersistenceContext
Interface to represent a persistence context.

The persistence context is the collection of all the managed objects of an EntityManager. The main role of the persistence context is to make sure that a database entity object is represented by no more than one in-memory entity object within the same EntityManager. Every EntityManager manages its own persistence context. Therefore, a database object can be represented by different memory entity objects in different EntityManager instances.

In the context of LiteEntityManager, nothing is cached in the memory. and object of this class also holds the ownership details of the currently active entity manager.

Author:
Sudiptasish Chanda

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    destroy()
    Destroy this persistence context.
    LiteEntityManager
    em()
    Return the entity manager that is part of this persistence context.
    Object
    owner()
    Return the owner of the currently active txn, thus the entity manager.
    LitePersistenceContext
    parent()
    Return the parent context.

  • Method Details

    • em

      LiteEntityManager em()
      Return the entity manager that is part of this persistence context.
      Returns:
      LiteEntityManager

    • owner

      Object owner()
      Return the owner of the currently active txn, thus the entity manager. It just provides a reference of the object that started the txn.
      Returns:
      Object

    • parent

      LitePersistenceContext parent()
      Return the parent context. Sometimes it is possible for application component to start a new pragma transaction, even if one transaction is in progress. In which case the currently active persistence context has to be backed up, before creaating a new context. The new entity persistence context will thus become a child of it.
      Returns:
      LitePersistenceContext

    • destroy

      void destroy()
      Destroy this persistence context.
